Load Libraries and Data

Data Visualizations

Welfare Trade-off Ratio by Donation Amount and Endowment Knowledge

df %>%
  group_by(prior, donation_amount, knows_endowment) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_wtr = mean(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_wtr = sd(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
ggplot(
       aes(x = donation_amount,
           y = mean_wtr,
           color = knows_endowment,
           group = knows_endowment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +         # <--- replaced size with linewidth
  geom_point(size = 3) +             # points still use size
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_wtr - se_wtr,
                    ymax = mean_wtr + se_wtr),
                width = 2,
                linewidth = 0.8) +  # error bars also use linewidth now
  facet_wrap(~ prior) +
  labs(
    x = "Donation Amount",
    y = "Mean Welfare Tradeoff Rating",
    color = "Knows Endowment",
    title = "Welfare Tradeoff Rating by Donation Amount\nLine Plot by Endowment Knowledge"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

Welfare Trade-off Ratio by Donation Percentage and Endowment Knowledge

df %>%
  group_by(prior, donation_percentage, knows_endowment) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_wtr = mean(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_wtr = sd(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
ggplot(
       aes(x = donation_percentage,
           y = mean_wtr,
           color = knows_endowment,
           group = knows_endowment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +        
  geom_point(size = 3) +             
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_wtr - se_wtr,
                    ymax = mean_wtr + se_wtr),
                width = 2,
                linewidth = 0.8) +  
  facet_wrap(~ prior) +
  labs(
    x = "Donation Percent",
    y = "Mean Welfare Tradeoff Rating",
    color = "Knows Endowment",
    title = "Welfare Tradeoff Rating by Donation Percentage\nLine Plot by Endowment Knowledge"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

Welfare Trade-off Ratio by Donation Percentage and faceted by Prior and Endowment Knowledge

# Convert ball value to factor 
df %>%
  mutate(ball_value = factor(ball_value)) |>
# Summarize data with grouping including ball_drawn
  group_by(prior, knows_endowment, donation_percentage, ball_value) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_wtr = mean(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_wtr = sd(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
# Plot
ggplot(
       aes(x = donation_percentage,
           y = mean_wtr,
           color = ball_value,
           group = ball_value)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_wtr - se_wtr,
                    ymax = mean_wtr + se_wtr),
                width = 2,
                linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(prior ~ knows_endowment) +  
  labs(
    x = "Donation Percentage",
    y = "Mean Welfare Tradeoff Rating",
    color = "Ball Drawn",
    title = "Welfare Tradeoff Rating by Donation Percentage\nFaceted by Prior and Endowment Knowledge"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

#### Same plot but with empathy instead of WTR ###

# Convert ball value to factor 
df %>%
  mutate(ball_value = factor(ball_value)) |>
# Summarize data with grouping including ball_drawn
  group_by(prior, knows_endowment, donation_percentage, ball_value) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_empathy = mean(empathy_rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_empathy = sd(empathy_rating, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
#Plot
ggplot(
       aes(x = donation_percentage,
           y = mean_empathy,
           color = ball_value,
           group = ball_value)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_empathy - se_empathy,
                    ymax = mean_empathy + se_empathy),
                width = 2,
                linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(prior ~ knows_endowment) +   
  labs(
    x = "Donation Percentage",
    y = "Mean Empathy Rating",
    color = "Ball Drawn",
    title = " Empathy Rating by Donation Percentage\nFaceted by Prior and Endowment Knowledge"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

Welfare Trade-off Ratio by Donation Percentage and faceted by Prior and Endowment Knowledge

# Convert ball value to factor 
df %>%
  mutate(ball_value = factor(ball_value)) |>
# Summarize data with grouping including ball_drawn
  group_by(prior, knows_endowment, donation_amount, ball_value) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_welfare_tradeoff = mean(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_welfare_tradeoff = sd(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
# Plot
ggplot(
       aes(x = donation_amount,
           y = mean_welfare_tradeoff,
           color = ball_value,
           group = ball_value)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_welfare_tradeoff - se_welfare_tradeoff,
                    ymax = mean_welfare_tradeoff + se_welfare_tradeoff),
                width = 2,
                linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(prior ~ knows_endowment) +   # facet rows = prior, cols = knows_endowment
  labs(
    x = "Donation Amount",
    y = "Mean Welfare Tradeoff Rating",
    color = "Ball Drawn",
    title = " Welfare Tradeoff Rating by Donation Amount\nFaceted by Prior and Endowment Knowledge"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

## Same Plot but with Empathy instead of WTR ## 

# Convert ball value to factor 
df %>%
  mutate(ball_value = factor(ball_value)) |>
# Summarize data with grouping including ball_drawn
  group_by(prior, knows_endowment, donation_amount, ball_value) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_empathy = mean(empathy_rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_empathy = sd(empathy_rating, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
# Plot
ggplot(
       aes(x = donation_amount,
           y = mean_empathy,
           color = ball_value,
           group = ball_value)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_empathy - se_empathy,
                    ymax = mean_empathy + se_empathy),
                width = 2,
                linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(prior ~ knows_endowment) +   # facet rows = prior, cols = knows_endowment
  labs(
    x = "Donation Amount",
    y = "Mean Empathy Rating",
    color = "Ball Drawn",
    title = "Empathy Rating by Donation Amount\nFaceted by Prior and Endowment Knowledge"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

Interaction between Endowment and Endowment Knowledge

df %>%
  filter(
    donation_amount >= 15,
    donation_amount <= 25,
    prior == "poor",
    ball_value == "100"
  ) %>%
  select(pid, knows_endowment, donation_amount, welfare_tradeoff_rating) |>
  group_by(donation_amount, knows_endowment) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_wtr = mean(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_wtr = sd(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
## Interaction Visualization
ggplot(aes(x = donation_amount, y = mean_wtr, color = knows_endowment)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.5) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_wtr - se_wtr, ymax = mean_wtr + se_wtr),
                width = 0.5, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Donation Amount",
    y = "Mean Welfare Tradeoff Rating",
    color = "Knows Endowment",
    title = "Interaction of Endowment Knowledge and Donation Amount\n(Poor Prior, $100 Ball)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
  )
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

Welfare Tradeoff Ratio by Donation Amount and Prior faceted by Endowment

#Convert Prior to Factor
df %>%
  mutate(prior = as.factor(prior)) |>
# Summarize data with grouping including ball_drawn
  group_by(prior, knows_endowment, donation_amount, ball_value) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_wtr = mean(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_wtr = sd(welfare_tradeoff_rating, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
#Plot
ggplot(
  aes(
    x = donation_amount,
    y = mean_wtr,
    color = knows_endowment,
    group = interaction(knows_endowment, prior),
    linetype = prior
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_wtr - se_wtr,
      ymax = mean_wtr + se_wtr
    ),
    width = 2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("dashed", "solid")) +
  facet_wrap(~ ball_value) +
  labs(
    x = "Donation Amount",
    y = "Mean Welfare Tradeoff Rating",
    color = "Knowledge",
    linetype = "Prior",
    title = "Welfare Tradeoff Rating by Donation Amount\nFaceted by Prior and Endowment Knowledge"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

Statistical Models for Preregistered Hypotheses

Hypothesis 1: Participant judgments about the actor’s welfare trade-off ratio will be correlated with the model predictions about the actor’s welfare trade-off ratio

## Add in correlation between model and participant judgements here

Hypothesis 2: Controlling for the amount of money shared, a prior belief about the giver having a high endowment will have a negative main effect on judgments about the welfare trade-off ratio

mod2 <- lmer(welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ donation_amount + prior + (1 | pid), data = df  )
summary(mod2)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ donation_amount + prior + (1 | pid)
##    Data: df
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 11611
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.3624 -0.7359 -0.1085  0.6145  2.7410 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  pid      (Intercept) 0.1959   0.4426  
##  Residual             2.3670   1.5385  
## Number of obs: 3096, groups:  pid, 129
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)      3.1300284  0.0674526  46.403
## donation_amount  0.0476600  0.0009008  52.911
## priorrich       -0.0899877  0.0966181  -0.931
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) dntn_m
## donatin_mnt -0.354       
## priorrich   -0.611  0.000

Hypothesis 3: Controlling for the amount of money shared, knowing the giver has a high endowment will have a negative main effect on judgments about the welfare trade-off ratio

mod3 <- lmer(welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ donation_amount + knows_endowment + (1 | pid), data = df)
summary(mod3)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ donation_amount + knows_endowment +  
##     (1 | pid)
##    Data: df
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 11561.1
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.5032 -0.7469 -0.1082  0.6676  2.6248 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  pid      (Intercept) 0.1973   0.4442  
##  Residual             2.3267   1.5253  
## Number of obs: 3096, groups:  pid, 129
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                      Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)         2.8933411  0.0599365  48.273
## donation_amount     0.0476600  0.0008931  53.366
## knows_endowmenttrue 0.3966408  0.0548275   7.234
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) dntn_m
## donatin_mnt -0.395       
## knws_ndwmnt -0.457  0.000

Hypothesis 4: Controlling for prior beliefs about endowment and the actual endowment, sharing money will have a positive main effect on judgments about the welfare trade-off ratio

mod4 <- lmer(welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ prior + ball_value + donation_amount + (1 | pid), data = df)
summary(mod4)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ prior + ball_value + donation_amount +  
##     (1 | pid)
##    Data: df
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 11268.2
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.6590 -0.7448 -0.0964  0.7047  3.5585 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  pid      (Intercept) 0.207    0.455   
##  Residual             2.101    1.449   
## Number of obs: 3096, groups:  pid, 129
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)      3.7505762  0.0742066  50.542
## priorrich       -0.0899877  0.0966181  -0.931
## ball_value      -0.0154651  0.0007963 -19.422
## donation_amount  0.0592585  0.0010376  57.109
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) prrrch bll_vl
## priorrich   -0.555              
## ball_value  -0.431  0.000       
## donatin_mnt  0.000  0.000 -0.576

Hypothesis 5: There will be an interaction between knowledge about the endowment and the amount of money the giver shares

## Model with all donation amounts 
mod5 <- lmer(welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ knows_endowment * donation_amount + (1 | pid), data = df)
summary(mod5)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ knows_endowment * donation_amount +  
##     (1 | pid)
##    Data: df
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 11560.6
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.39179 -0.73964 -0.09506  0.66959  2.57740 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  pid      (Intercept) 0.1976   0.4445  
##  Residual             2.3186   1.5227  
## Number of obs: 3096, groups:  pid, 129
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                      Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)                          2.813729   0.064389  43.699
## knows_endowmenttrue                  0.555866   0.072306   7.688
## donation_amount                      0.050664   0.001261  40.184
## knows_endowmenttrue:donation_amount -0.006008   0.001783  -3.370
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) knws_n dntn_m
## knws_ndwmnt -0.561              
## donatin_mnt -0.519  0.462       
## knws_ndwm:_  0.367 -0.653 -0.707
## Model looking at the interaction specifically between $15 and $25
df_subset <- df %>%
  filter(
    donation_amount >= 15,
    donation_amount <= 25,
    prior == "poor",
    ball_value == "100"
  ) %>%
  select(pid, knows_endowment, donation_amount, welfare_tradeoff_rating)

mod5a <- lmer(welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ knows_endowment * donation_amount + (1 | pid), data = df_subset)
summary(mod5a)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ knows_endowment * donation_amount +  
##     (1 | pid)
##    Data: df_subset
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 791.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.39636 -0.62313 -0.02873  0.49999  3.02844 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  pid      (Intercept) 0.8868   0.9417  
##  Residual             0.4704   0.6859  
## Number of obs: 296, groups:  pid, 74
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                      Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)                          4.243243   0.256942   16.51
## knows_endowmenttrue                 -2.317568   0.328742   -7.05
## donation_amount                      0.002703   0.011276    0.24
## knows_endowmenttrue:donation_amount  0.077027   0.015946    4.83
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) knws_n dntn_m
## knws_ndwmnt -0.640              
## donatin_mnt -0.878  0.686       
## knws_ndwm:_  0.621 -0.970 -0.707

Hypothesis 6: There will be a positive correlation between judgments about empathy and judgments about the welfare trade-off ratio

cor.test(df$welfare_tradeoff_rating, df$empathy_rating)
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  df$welfare_tradeoff_rating and df$empathy_rating
## t = 229.75, df = 3094, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.9699023 0.9738078
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.9719219

Exploratory Statistical Models

Exploratory 1: Controlling for donation amount ($15) and knowledge about endowment (unknown), prior beliefs about endowment will have an effect on the welfare trade-off ratio

df_exploratory_1 <- df %>%
  filter(
    donation_amount <= 20,
    knows_endowment == "false"
  )

mod_e1 <- lmer(welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ donation_amount + prior + (1 | pid), data = df_exploratory_1  )
summary(mod_e1)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ donation_amount + prior + (1 | pid)
##    Data: df_exploratory_1
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 3009.9
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.9897 -0.6235 -0.1009  0.5802  3.8365 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  pid      (Intercept) 0.4752   0.6894  
##  Residual             0.8665   0.9309  
## Number of obs: 1032, groups:  pid, 129
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)      1.840592   0.095296  19.315
## donation_amount  0.176147   0.004067  43.306
## priorrich       -0.221929   0.136000  -1.632
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) dntn_m
## donatin_mnt -0.363       
## priorrich   -0.608  0.000

Exploratory 2: Three-way interaction between Prior x Endowment Knowledge x Endowment

df_exploratory_2 <- df %>%
  filter(
    donation_amount >= 15,
    donation_amount <= 25
  ) %>%
  select(pid, knows_endowment, donation_amount, welfare_tradeoff_rating, prior, ball_value)

# Ensure prior and knows_endowment are factors
df_exploratory_2$prior <- factor(df_exploratory_2$prior, levels = c("rich", "poor"))
df_exploratory_2$knows_endowment <- factor(df_exploratory_2$knows_endowment, levels = c("true", "false"))

mod_e2 <- lmer(welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ prior * knows_endowment * donation_amount + (1 | pid), data = df_exploratory_2)
summary(mod_e2)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: welfare_tradeoff_rating ~ prior * knows_endowment * donation_amount +  
##     (1 | pid)
##    Data: df_exploratory_2
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 3878.8
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.5263 -0.6919  0.0234  0.7139  1.8700 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  pid      (Intercept) 0.3056   0.5528  
##  Residual             2.2343   1.4948  
## Number of obs: 1032, groups:  pid, 129
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                                 Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)                                     5.818182   0.472699  12.308
## priorpoor                                       0.079853   0.624114   0.128
## knows_endowmentfalse                           -1.231405   0.660135  -1.865
## donation_amount                                -0.040000   0.024308  -1.646
## priorpoor:knows_endowmentfalse                 -0.272281   0.871588  -0.312
## priorpoor:donation_amount                      -0.004472   0.032094  -0.139
## knows_endowmentfalse:donation_amount            0.024463   0.034377   0.712
## priorpoor:knows_endowmentfalse:donation_amount  0.024923   0.045388   0.549
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) prirpr knws_n dntn_m prrpr:k_ prrpr:d_ knw_:_
## priorpoor   -0.757                                              
## knws_ndwmnt -0.698  0.529                                       
## donatin_mnt -0.964  0.730  0.690                                
## prrpr:knws_  0.529 -0.698 -0.757 -0.523                         
## prrpr:dntn_  0.730 -0.964 -0.523 -0.757  0.690                  
## knws_ndwm:_  0.682 -0.516 -0.976 -0.707  0.740    0.536         
## prrpr:kn_:_ -0.516  0.682  0.740  0.536 -0.976   -0.707   -0.757
